articles.title: Russian President Putin meets with China's Xi Jinping, days after Trump's visit
articles.summary: President Vladimir Putin is in Beijing for talks with President Xi Jinping, in a closely watched visit that comes just days after President Trump's high-profile trip to China.

articles.title: Xi and Putin meet to reaffirm China-Russia ties days after Trump's visit
articles.summary: Russian President Vladimir Putin praised his close ties with Chinese leader Xi Jinping and said their countries are partners as they opened bilateral talks Wednesday on his trip to Beijing.

articles.title: 'Taiwan Travelogue' wins the 2026 International Booker Prize
articles.summary: The novel is the first work translated from Mandarin Chinese to win the award, which celebrates its 10th anniversary this year.

articles.title: Iran Threatens to Strike Beyond the Middle East if the U.S. Resumes Attacks
articles.summary: The warning was issued as President Trump and Vice President JD Vance say progress is being made toward a deal, while keeping open the threat of renewed strikes.

articles.title: Former Spanish Prime Minister Zapatero is under investigation
articles.summary: A Spanish court is investigating former Prime Minister José Luis Rodríguez Zapatero for alleged influence peddling and other possible crimes tied to a government airline bailout.

articles.title: New Immunity for President Trump, and an Audacious Plan for Regime Change in Iran
articles.summary: Plus, Tesla’s big bet on big rigs.

articles.title: Prison to Pardons to Payouts: Jan. 6 Rioters Are Elated at Trump Compensation Fund
articles.summary: The possibility that people who ransacked the Capitol could get money from the government they attacked is the latest head-spinning twist in President Trump’s effort to rewrite the history of Jan. 6.

articles.title: Overworked and understaffed: Special ed teachers turn to AI for help
articles.summary: A fast-growing number of special educators nationwide are using AI to create customized education plans. Despite the risks, some research shows it could improve the quality of teachers' work.

articles.title: Bees have coexisted with us for over a millennia. Their name remains a mystery
articles.summary: Its name is short — like its size — but bees are one of Earth's most important and busy creatures.

articles.title: Gun rights groups see 'golden age' under Trump administration
articles.summary: New gun rules and lawsuits against states signal how this Trump administration is using its powers to align itself with the wishes of the gun rights movement.

articles.title: 4 takeaways from Tuesday's primary night in half a dozen states
articles.summary: While Trump continues to rack up victories on his vengeance tour, general election opponents are waiting in swing districts and swing states. Can front-line GOP candidates navigate these choppy waters

articles.title: Denver has a plan to heat and cool buildings without fossil fuels. It involves … sewage?
articles.summary: Like many cities, Denver's largest source of greenhouse gas emissions is its buildings. Heating and cooling skyscrapers requires a lot of fossil fuels. Now, the city is trying a surprising solution.

articles.title: Morning news brief
articles.summary: A look at primary results from around the country, Trump and Vance share conflicting messages on state of Iran war, shooting at San Diego mosque being investigated as possible "hate crime."

articles.title: Montana could soon ease restrictions on mining precious metals
articles.summary: Restrictions on mining for precious metals like gold in Montana that were put in place 30 years ago could soon be relaxed as the mining industry makes a comeback in the U.S.

articles.title: Report finds global aid system is failing to address today's humanitarian crises
articles.summary: A new report finds that global humanitarian aid is failing, with cuts from major donors like the United States and increased attacks on health workers further eroding the system.

articles.title: Bolivia's capital under siege as protests deepen crisis for President Paz
articles.summary: Bolivia's President Rodrigo Paz faces a deepening crisis as widespread protests and blockades leave the political capital under siege less than six months after he took office.

articles.title: Early War Goal Was to Install Hard Line Former President as Iran’s Leader
articles.summary: An Israeli strike designed to free Mahmoud Ahmadinejad from house arrest in Tehran, U.S. officials said, was part of an effort to bring about regime change and put him in power.

articles.title: WHO chief concerned over 'scale and speed' of Ebola outbreak
articles.summary: The World Health Organization top official has expressed concern over the rapid spread of a rare type of Ebola in Congo. Authorities have reported at least 134 suspected deaths and over 500 cases.

articles.title: I.R.S. to Drop Audits of Trump and Family
articles.summary: As part of the Justice Department’s compensation fund deal, officials vowed not to pursue any matters, including those involving President Trump’s tax returns, that are pending.

articles.title: With Trump’s Settlement, a Possible $100 Million I.R.S. Penalty Melts Away
articles.summary: The tax service argued that the Trump Organization tried to claim the same losses twice. The president said the audit was a “disgrace.”

articles.title: Senate Votes to Take Up Measure to Force Trump to End Iran War
articles.summary: With four Republican backers, Democrats won a vote to advance a resolution that would force the president to end hostilities or win authorization from Congress.

articles.title: The IRS Thought it Could Fight Trump’s Lawsuit, but it Struck a Deal Anyway
articles.summary: Officials wrote a memo outlining ways to challenge President Trump’s suit against the Internal Revenue Service. The administration is instead creating a compensation fund.

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
